PRODUCT: Sul2 is a sulfonamide resistant dihydropteroate synthase of Gram-negative bacteria usually found on small plasmids.
ANT(3'')-IIa is a aminoglycoside nucleotidyltransferase identified in Acinetobacter spp. via horizontal gene transfer mechanisms.
AbeM is an multidrug efflux pump found in Acinetobacter baumannii.
LpsB is involved in lipopolysaccharide synthesis. It confers intrinsic resistance to colistin and other peptide antibiotics.
Expression of abaF in E. coli resulted in increased resistance to fosfomycin.
OXA-51 family carbapenem-hydrolyzing class D beta-lactamase OXA-1117.
AdeB is the multidrug transporter of the adeABC efflux system.
AdeA is the membrane fusion protein of the multidrug efflux complex AdeABC.
AdeR is a positive regulator of AdeABC efflux system. AdeR inactivation leads to susceptibility to aminoglycoside antibiotics.
AdeS is a sensor kinase in the AdeRS regulatory system of AdeABC. It is essential for AdeABC expression.
AbaQ is an MFS transporter mainly involved in the extrusion of quinolone-type drugs in A. baumannii.
AdeN is a repressor of AdeIJK a RND-type efflux pump in Acinetobacter baumannii. Its inactivation increases expression of AdeJ.

AmvA has 14 alpha-helical transmembrane segments qualifying it as a member of the DHA2 transporter family of the major facilitator superfamily (MFS). When AmvA was expressed in E. coli  the cells had at least four-fold decreased susceptibility to erythromycin acridine orange acriflavine deoxycholate and methyl viologen. AmvA from the AC0037 strain of A. baumannii was tested.
AbeS in an efflux pump of the SMR family of transporters found in Acinetobacter baumannii.
AdeL is a regulator of AdeFGH in Acinetobacter baumannii. AdeL mutations are associated with AdeFGH overexpression and multidrug resistance.
AdeG is the inner membrane transporter of the AdeFGH multidrug efflux complex.
AdeF is the membrane fusion protein of the multidrug efflux complex AdeFGH.
AdeH is the outer membrane channel protein of the AdeFGH multidrug efflux complex.
ADC-158 is a ADC beta-lactamase.
AdeI is the membrane fusion protein of the AdeIJK multidrug efflux complex.
AdeJ is a RND efflux protein that acts as the inner membrane transporter of the AdeIJK efflux complex. It has 57% identity with E. coli AcrB.
AdeK is the outer membrane factor protein in the adeIJK multidrug efflux complex.
